img_intro_paragraph: We took a Sumo Wrestling tour in Ryogoku, the historic heart of Japanese sumo wrestling, famous for the Ryogoku Kokugikan National Sumo Stadium, which hosts major tournaments. The area hosts many sumo stables where wrestlers live and train, often seen walking around in yukatas. Unfortunately while we were there the team was out of town so we didn't see any wrestlers.
img_title: November 24, 2025 - Sumo Wrestling Tour Tokyo Japan

img_caption: Ryogoku District Sumo Themed Manhole Cover
img_intro_paragraph: The district sports special manhole covers sporting the sumo wrestler mascot characters Hiyonoyama and Akawashi.

We weren't allowed to view the Ryogoku Kokugikan Sumo Arena the day of our tour, so instead we visited this nearby food hall located near the arena. It sports a full sized dohyo, the sumo wrestling ring.

img_caption: Sumo Wrestler Statue Near Ryogoku Kokugikan Arena

img_intro_paragraph: We did a self guided tour to the Tokyo Skytree which has a viewing area 350 floors up.

We finally boarded the cruise that would take us to Hong Kong with a number of stops in Japan, Taiwan and the Philippines. Originally the cruise was scheduled to end in Manila, which was the main reason we took it. However about a month before we deparedt for Korea, Norwegian canceled the stop in Manila and ended the tour in Hong Kong. Luckily the flight from Hong Kong to Manila is fairly short and not too expensive. However the last minute change and their refusal to reimburse us has made me hesitant to book Norwegian cruises again.

We had boarded the ship in Tokyo but our first stop was not far away in the Shimizu Port, which is near Mount Fuji. Local areas raise trout and horseradish for wasabi all with fresh clear water from Mount Fuji. We lucked out again with beautiful views of Mount Fuji both during the tour and from the dock inf Shimizu Port.

img_caption: Jinba Falls Mount Fuji Fed Water

img_intro_paragraph: In Osaka we took a private walking tour of Osaka Castle and Shinsekai, Dotonboriand surrounding areas. The manhole cover below was to commemorate an upcoming expo.
At this cruise ship stop we took the Ultimate Osaka Walking Tour.
This hotel in Osaka offered hourly rates and two entrances and exits. Why two? So no one could photograph you leaving from the same entrances as some strange (not your wife/gf) woman.
In Kochi we took the Kochi Highlights tour.

img_title: Dec 1, 2024 - Amami Oshima Highlights,  Oshima Japan

img_caption: Cal La Young Garden Resort  Hot Springs
img_intro_paragraph: Our tour this day took us to Yangmingshan Natl Park and nearby Cal La Young Garden Resort Hot Springs. Unfortunately bad weather kept us from seeing much at Yangmingshan, the only park in Taiwan that has volcanic geography and hot springs.
We'd been to Taiwan before but never to Kaohsiung. There were a lot of interesting sights and places to visit. We first went to Ten Drum Creative Park, a former Sugar Mill.
This day we were on our own but managed to find a ride for a reasonable price just outside the port.
For our day in Coron we had a private all day tour that included snorkeling. Click the picture below to see more underwater photos.

img_caption:
View From Hike Leading to 
Kayangan Lake

img_intro_paragraph: Because the cruise ended in Hong Kong instead of Manila, we had and over night in Hong Kong. That allowed us to take a private half day tour of Hong Kong highlights.
